member.name: T:YasGMP.Models.Attachment
member.summary:
Attachment – Ultra-robust digital record for any file (PDF, image, scan, doc, certificate, etc.) linked to GMP/CMMS entities (WorkOrder, Machine, Component, CAPA, Calibration, Inspection, etc).
✅ Fully auditable (upload, approval, expiry, rollback, versioning, chain-of-custody, e-sign).
✅ Supports multi-entity links, digital signature, hash, OCR, session, device, IP, ML/AI.
✅ Inspector, regulatory, and future-proof: ALL FIELDS for forensic, analytics, rollback, audit, and more.

UI-facing metadata extensions surfaced when projecting attachments from raw SQL queries. These properties are not mapped to EF columns but allow binding layers to expose retention/legal-hold information without relying on legacy values.
Schema-level extensions for ensuring every database column is surfaced.

member.summary: User-friendly display name or description of the file (not the file name).

member.summary: Actual file name on disk, cloud, or original upload (used for download/display).

member.summary: File storage path, URL, or blob identifier (location on disk/cloud/DB).

member.summary: File extension/type (pdf, jpg, docx, etc.).

member.summary: File size in bytes (optional, for quotas/security checks).

member.summary: Entity type this attachment is linked to (e.g., "WorkOrder", "Machine").

member.summary: ID of the linked entity (e.g., WorkOrderId, MachineId, etc).

member.name: T:YasGMP.Models.AuditLog
member.summary:
AuditLog – Central system audit/activity log for every user and system action (CRUD, login, export, print, rollback, digital signature, etc.).
Fully GMP, 21 CFR Part 11, EU Annex 11, and HALMED compliant.
Tracks: who, when, what action, what data/entity, what field, before/after values, device, IP, context, severity, digital signature, and rollback version.
• Every change (incl. failed attempts) must be logged here for regulatory, forensics, and legal defense.
• All digital signature events should be cryptographically validated and traceable.
• Use for all CRUD, user, integration, and critical system actions!
Schema-level augmentation for bridging all columns surfaced by the underlying MySQL view (audit_log).

member.name: P:YasGMP.Models.AuditLog.Id
member.summary: Unique log entry ID (primary key, auto-increment).

member.name: T:YasGMP.Models.DelegatedPermission
member.summary:
DelegatedPermission – Forensic, GMP/21 CFR Part 11 compliant record describing a permission delegated from one user to another (proxy, escalation, coverage, incident handling, RBAC+).
• Designed for MAUI + MVVM + MySQL and Entity Framework Core.
• Captures: delegator/delegatee, window (start/end), approver, reason, device/IP/session, digital signature, active/revoked state, and change version.
• Intended to be the single authoritative EF entity (avoid duplicate class definitions).
✅ To prevent compiler errors CS0101, CS0229, and CS0579, ensure there is only one EF entity class named in the YasGMP.Models namespace. If you keep any additional partials, they must not redeclare properties nor repeat the .
Schema-level expansion for covering legacy timestamp columns and labels.

member.name: T:YasGMP.Models.Deviation
member.summary:
Deviation – Ultra-mega robust GMP deviation/non-conformance record (audit, workflow, root cause, CAPA, digital signature, AI-ready).
Supports complete GMP/Annex 11/21 CFR Part 11 requirements, forensics, workflow, advanced escalation, custom fields, attachments, and AI/ML hooks.
Schema-level extensions for exposing raw database columns and serialised helpers.

member.name: T:YasGMP.Models.DeviationAudit
member.summary: Ultra-robust GMP audit entry for a single deviation, aligned to the deviation_audit SQL table. Maps one row per action (CREATE/UPDATE/APPROVE/REJECT/ASSIGN/ESCALATE/EXPORT/ROLLBACK/etc.), including forensics.
member.remarks: SQL origin: table deviation_audit in YASGMP.sql. Columns are mirrored 1:1 where possible. Extra enum wrappers are provided for readability/type-safety while retaining string persistence when needed.

member.name: T:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CalibrationResult
member.summary:
CalibrationResult – Standardized outcome of any calibration or metrological verification process.
Usage: Used in , , reports, and audit logs. Supports GMP/GLP/ISO 17025 digital traceability.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CalibrationResult.Pass
member.summary: Calibration completed successfully; all values within tolerance/spec.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CalibrationResult.Fail
member.summary: Calibration failed; at least one value outside of tolerance/spec.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CalibrationResult.Conditional
member.summary: Conditional pass – valid only under specific conditions or temporary (e.g., used with a note).

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CalibrationResult.Note
member.summary: Calibration completed, but operator/inspector added a special note or warning.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CalibrationResult.NotPerformed
member.summary: Calibration was not possible due to missing equipment, access, or external factors.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CalibrationResult.ReverifyRequired
member.summary: Calibration requires repeat/verification (e.g., environmental issue, disputed result).

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CalibrationResult.PendingReview
member.summary: Calibration data uploaded digitally (e.g. via IoT device, cloud, LIMS), pending review.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CalibrationResult.Archived
member.summary: Calibration result is obsolete/superseded (used for versioning/archiving).

member.name: T:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CapaActionType
member.summary:
CapaActionType – Enumerates all standardized action types for CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Actions) audit logging.
Used in CAPA modules, audit trails, regulatory reporting, and CAPA workflow automation. Fully aligned with GMP, ISO 13485, ICH Q10, and 21 CFR Part 11. Extensible for any future CAPA/QA/CSV scenario!

member.name: T:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CapaStatus
member.summary:
CapaStatus – Status for Corrective and Preventive Actions (CAPA) cases, fully GMP/ISO/21 CFR Part 11 compliant.
Used in , , dashboards, workflow automation, and compliance reports. Includes all legacy, UI, and extensibility aliases for maximum compatibility.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CapaStatus.Open
member.summary: CAPA is open and requires attention/action.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CapaStatus.InProgress
member.summary: CAPA is under investigation, assignment, or active remediation.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CapaStatus.AwaitingApproval
member.summary: CAPA is pending management or QA approval/signoff before closure or implementation.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CapaStatus.Closed
member.summary: CAPA has been fully resolved and closed after verification of effectiveness.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CapaStatus.Cancelled
member.summary: CAPA was cancelled or invalidated (GMP justification required, always audited).

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CapaStatus.Archived
member.summary: CAPA is archived (inactive, for records/forensic trace).

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CapaStatus.Reopened
member.summary: Previously closed CAPA is re-opened for further actions or follow-up.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CapaStatus.Overdue
member.summary: CAPA is overdue (missed due date, escalated for review/action).

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CapaStatus.OnHold
member.summary: CAPA on hold, e.g., pending input from third party or external investigation.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CapaStatus.EffectivenessCheck
member.summary: CAPA is under effectiveness review period (outcome pending).

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CapaStatus.Escalated
member.summary: CAPA escalated to management, audit, or regulatory authority.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CapaStatus.Investigation
member.summary: Status for any CAPA that is actively being investigated.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CapaStatus.Created
member.summary: Status for any CAPA that has just been created and is in the open state.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CapaStatus.ActionDefined
member.summary: Status for CAPA that is under action definition (plan created but not yet executed).

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CapaStatus.ActionExecuted
member.summary: Status for CAPA where action has been executed (implementation phase).

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CapaStatus.ActionApproved
member.summary: Status for CAPA that is awaiting formal approval (QA, management, regulator).

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CapaStatus.Verification
member.summary: Status for CAPA currently being verified for effectiveness.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.CapaStatus.Complete
member.summary: Status for CAPA marked as complete and closed.

member.summary:
DeviationActionType – All possible actions/events in deviation (non-conformance) lifecycle for audit/logging.
Used in and throughout the DeviationService and deviation management workflow.
Fully extensible for regulatory compliance, analytics, AI/ML, and reporting.

member.summary: Deviation record created (initial entry).

member.name: T:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DeviationStatus
member.summary:
DeviationStatus — All workflow states for deviations/non-conformances.
Follows full GMP/Annex 11/ICH Q10/21 CFR Part 11 workflow: OPEN → INVESTIGATION → ROOT_CAUSE → CAPA_LINKED → CLOSED (+ extras for full tracking)

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DeviationStatus.OPEN
member.summary: Newly reported, not yet assigned or investigated.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DeviationStatus.INVESTIGATION
member.summary: Investigation in progress (assigned to investigator).

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DeviationStatus.ROOT_CAUSE
member.summary: Root cause defined/documented.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DeviationStatus.CAPA_LINKED
member.summary: Linked to CAPA for corrective/preventive actions.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DeviationStatus.CLOSED
member.summary: Deviation closed with justification and QA signoff.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DeviationStatus.ON_HOLD
member.summary: On hold/waiting for info (optional, for advanced workflow).

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DeviationStatus.ESCALATED
member.summary: Escalated (QA/Management review required).

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DeviationStatus.INVALIDATED
member.summary: Invalidated (not a true deviation after review).

member.name: T:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DigitalSignatureMethod

DigitalSignatureMethod – Enumerates all methods for digital or electronic signatures in GMP/CSV/21 CFR Part 11 contexts.
Fully auditable, extensible for any authentication/signature scheme.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DigitalSignatureMethod.Certificate

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DigitalSignatureMethod.Password

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DigitalSignatureMethod.API

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DigitalSignatureMethod.MobileApp

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DigitalSignatureMethod.SMS_OTP

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DigitalSignatureMethod.Manual

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DigitalSignatureMethod.HardwareToken
member.summary: Hardware token (YubiKey, USB token, etc).

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DigitalSignatureMethod.Other
member.summary: Other/custom (for new signature standards).

member.name: T:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DocumentStatus
member.summary:
DocumentStatus – Workflow status for any controlled document, SOP, or record in the system.
Fully supports versioning, archiving, GMP audit, digital signature, and compliance checks.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DocumentStatus.Draft
member.summary: Draft document – not yet approved.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DocumentStatus.UnderReview

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DocumentStatus.Active
member.summary: Active (approved and current version).

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DocumentStatus.Obsolete
member.summary: Obsolete (replaced by a new version, no longer used).

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DocumentStatus.Archived

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DocumentStatus.Invalidated

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DocumentStatus.Superseded
member.summary: Superseded by a newer version.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DocumentStatus.PendingApproval

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DocumentStatus.Rejected

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.DocumentStatus.Other

member.name: T:YasGMP.Models.Enums.EventSeverity
member.summary:
EventSeverity – Classification of the severity/importance of system, audit, security, and compliance events.
Usage: Used in system event logs, audit trails, notification systems, dashboard alerts, SIEM integrations, and forensic compliance reports.
Compliant with: FDA 21 CFR Part 11, EU GMP Annex 11, ISO 27001, GAMP5.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.EventSeverity.Info
member.summary: Informational event – normal system operation, no action required.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.EventSeverity.Warning
member.summary: Warning event – potentially problematic or abnormal event, but not critical.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.EventSeverity.Critical
member.summary: Critical event – urgent attention required; system failure, safety, data loss, regulatory risk.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.EventSeverity.Audit
member.summary: Audit event – regulatory, compliance, or internal audit event.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.EventSeverity.Security
member.summary: Security event – unauthorized access, attempted breach, authentication failure, or privilege misuse.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.EventSeverity.Forensic
member.summary: Forensic event – used for post-incident analysis, tamper detection, or legal chain-of-custody.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.EventSeverity.Debug
member.summary: Debug event – only visible in debug/developer mode for troubleshooting (not for production logs).

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.EventSeverity.Notification
member.summary: Notification – triggers user/system notifications (may be info, warning, or critical).

member.name: T:YasGMP.Models.Enums.ExportFormat
member.summary:
ExportFormat – Supported export, print, and reporting formats in YasGMP.
Used for document exports, report generation, audit trails, regulatory submissions, and interoperability.
Extensible for new digital standards and hybrid formats. Compliant with GxP, GMP, CSV, and FDA 21 CFR Part 11 requirements for traceable data output.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.ExportFormat.Excel
member.summary: Microsoft Excel spreadsheet (.xlsx, .xls).

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.ExportFormat.Pdf
member.summary: Portable Document Format (.pdf) – suitable for signed records, regulatory, and immutable reports.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.ExportFormat.Csv
member.summary: Comma-Separated Values (.csv) – machine-friendly, for import/export, analytics, and API.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.ExportFormat.Xml
member.summary: XML (eXtensible Markup Language) – for data exchange, ERP, and regulatory systems.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.ExportFormat.Json
member.summary: JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) – for API, IoT, and system-to-system integrations.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.ExportFormat.Other
member.summary: Other or custom/proprietary export formats (must specify via custom field).

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.IncidentSeverity.Other

MachineStatus – Comprehensive status for machines/equipment/assets in a GMP/CMMS environment.

member.name: T:YasGMP.Models.Enums.PermissionType
member.summary:
PermissionType – All possible granular actions available in the YasGMP system.
Used for RBAC (Role-Based Access Control), security, audit trail, menu/UI display, automation, and compliance mapping. Every action can be assigned to a role or a specific user, and logged for audit/compliance.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.PermissionType.Configure

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.PermissionType.Print

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.PermissionType.Restore

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.PermissionType.Audit

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.PermissionType.Download
member.summary: Can download files, reports, or data.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.PermissionType.Upload
member.summary: Can upload files or data.

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.PermissionType.Comment

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.PermissionType.Notify

member.name: F:YasGMP.Models.Enums.PermissionType.Custom

member.name: T:YasGMP.Models.Enums.UserRole
member.summary:
UserRole – All possible system roles for the GMP/CMMS/YasGMP platform.
Structured, extensible, and audit-ready role definitions.
Compliant with GMP, 21 CFR Part 11, CSV, Annex 11, FDA/EU.
To add a new role: 1. Add it here with XML summary. 2. Update the RBAC/permission mapping. 3. Sync workflow, audit, and UI code.

member.name: T:YasGMP.Models.Enums.UserRoleType
member.summary:
UserRoleType – UI- and logic-facing user roles for workflow, permissions, audit, and analytics.
- Used for system logic, RBAC, workflow routing, reporting, UI display (supports multi-language via ).
- Enables mapping to `UserRole` (technical), permissions, and auditing.
- GMP, CSV, Annex 11, and 21 CFR Part 11 ready.
- Designed for extensibility (add, localize, or map custom roles).
How to extend: Add new roles with for UI localization.
Keep Custom and reserved ranges for future-proofing and client-specific roles.

member.name: T:YasGMP.Models.Enums.ValidationStatus
member.summary: ValidationStatus – All possible states of a GMP validation/qualification record. Fully compliant with EU GMP Annex 15, ISO 13485, 21 CFR Part 11. Future-proof for digital workflow, audit, dashboard, reporting.

member.name: T:YasGMP.Models.DocumentControl
member.summary:
DocumentControl – Ultra-robust, audit-ready document/SOP master record for GMP/CMMS/QMS systems.
✅ Supports versioning, workflow, approvals, digital signatures, users/roles, and full traceability.
✅ Designed for 21 CFR Part 11, EU GMP Annex 11, HALMED, FDA, and ISO 9001/13485.
✅ Forensic logging: every change, review, approval, and digital event can be tracked and reconstructed.
✅ Extensible for electronic signatures, change control, lifecycle, and user-specific views.

AuditEntryDto – Ultra-robust Data Transfer Object for displaying and processing audit logs, forensics, and rollback entries.

ExternalContractor – Super ultra mega robust GMP/CMMS master record for every external contractor, service provider, or vendor.

item.description: Per-user, per-table, per-field access rules (read, write, approve, export, view history, digital sign, rollback, etc.)
Tracks who assigned/revoked, when, IP/device, justification, expiry, audit chain, soft delete, future extensibility.
21 CFR Part 11 & GMP bulletproof for AI/automation, fine-grained security, and full regulatory/forensic defense.

member.name: T:YasGMP.Models.MachineComponent
member.summary:
MachineComponent – Digital twin model for a machine/asset component.
• Full lifecycle tracking (calibration, validation, CAPA, docs, photos, signatures, IoT)
• Maximum traceability (audit, forensics, inspector/validation-ready)
• Ready for Industry 4.0 (AI/ML analytics, digital workflow, smart maintenance)
• Extended for forward compatibility with extra fields and relationships
Schema-level extensions for exposing raw table columns and legacy metadata blobs.

member.name: T:YasGMP.Models.Permission
member.summary:
Permission – Defines an atomic system right/ability for ultra-robust, forensic RBAC.
• Fully GMP/CSV/21 CFR Part 11/Annex 11 ready.
• Extensible, auditable, regulatory-tagged, and hierarchy-enabled.
• Supports compliance dashboards, risk analysis, audit, change tracking, and inspector reporting.
- Maps to roles via .
- Supports parent/child hierarchy and groupings.
- Add new permissions here and sync your UI, logic, and reporting modules.

member.summary: QualityEvent - Comprehensive GMP/CMMS record of quality-related events (deviations, complaints, recalls, OOS, change control, audit, training, etc.). Schema helpers keeping aligned with the underlying table while preserving the domain-focused surface.

member.name: T:YasGMP.Models.RiskAssessment
member.summary:
RiskAssessment – Ultra-robust record for GMP/CMMS/QMS risk analysis, mitigation, audit, and compliance.
✅ Tracks risk assessment events: title, category/area, detailed description, scoring (S×P×D), mitigation/action plan, owner/approval workflow, attachments and workflow history.
✅ Regulatory-ready (ICH Q9, 21 CFR Part 11, Annex 11, ISO 14971, HALMED).
✅ Full audit, digital signatures, escalation, document linkage, periodic review, forensics fields (IP/device/session).

member.name: T:YasGMP.Models.Role
member.summary:
Role – Ultra-robust, auditable user role for GMP/CSV/21 CFR/Annex 11/ITIL-compliant RBAC.
• Fully supports multi-level RBAC, workflow, escalation, legal/audit traceability, SoD (Segregation of Duties), delegation, and incident forensics.
• Used for permission mapping, user-role assignments, workflow routing, and regulatory reporting.
• Recommended: Map to (assignment), (access), and (role-right mapping).
- Extend for multi-org, jurisdiction, contract/vendor, and special regulatory modules.
- Safe for legal trace, incident defense, compliance, and audit dashboarding.
- All changes should be tracked via the audit log.

RolePermission – Assigns a to a (RBAC many-to-many mapping).
• Fully GMP/CSV/21 CFR Part 11/Annex 11/forensic/audit-ready.

StockChangeLog – Full audit, forensic, and analytics-ready log for every inventory movement, change, or event.

member.name: T:YasGMP.Models.SystemEventLog
member.summary: – Ultimate, ultra-robust GMP/CSV/Annex 11/21 CFR Part 11-compliant audit & event log. Tracks: who, what, when, where, how, old/new values, device, session, severity, AI/ML flags, compliance, chain-of-custody, rollback, digital signature, and more. Forensic, regulatory, and analytics ready!
Schema-level mappings for ensuring raw database columns are exposed alongside the domain-friendly surface.

member.summary:
TrainingRecord – Super ultra mega robust master record for employee training, GMP/CMMS compliance, and e-learning.
✅ Tracks all regulatory, technical, safety, SOP, and equipment trainings (internal/external, e-learning, on-site).
✅ Supports full linkage to users, roles, departments, trainers, training modules, SOPs, certificates, audit, and digital signatures.
✅ Forensic-ready: attendance, attachments, session history, skill matrix, expiry, re-training, escalation, and regulatory inspection.
✅ Extensible for e-learning (quiz, result, media), group/individual, workflow (approval, acknowledgment), multi-language, and mobile.
This class also exposes a set of [NotMapped] bridge properties so older/newer ViewModels with slightly different names compile without any schema change (e.g. Title → Name, TrainingType → Type, etc.).

member.name: T:YasGMP.Models.UserPermission
member.summary:
UserPermission – Maps a directly to a (RBAC override table).
• Ultra-robust: GMP, 21 CFR Part 11, CSV, forensics, escalation, digital signature, expiry, and device/session trace.
• Used for direct overrides (escalations, incident response, temporary access) that bypass role-based permission matrix.
• All grants, revokes, and changes should be logged in the audit trail for full legal and regulatory traceability.
- Enables granular, temporary, or emergency user rights for incident, investigation, or legal reasons.
- Ideal for highly regulated and forensic-grade environments.
- Supports audit, rollback, SOD, and regulator queries.
Schema extension for exposing grant metadata and legacy label columns.

member.summary: WorkOrderAttachment – Svi prilozi, dokumenti, slike, zapisi vezani za radni nalog. Svaki attachment je 100% auditiran, forenzički zaštićen (hash, watermark, potpis, tip, verzija, rollback). Omogućuje upload i vezu na PDF, slike, video, audio, e-mail, XML, Excel... member.name: T:YasGMP.Models.WorkOrderAudit
member.summary:
WorkOrderAudit – GMP/21 CFR Part 11 compliant forensic record for all work order actions.
✅ Tracks every CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, CLOSE, SIGN, EXPORT, and ROLLBACK action.
✅ Contains full forensic metadata (IP, device, session, timestamps) and cryptographic integrity mechanisms.
✅ Supports rollback, inspection, CAPA linkage, and audit trail compliance.
Schema augmentations for exposing additional columns from the database dump.

member.name: T:YasGMP.Services.BackgroundScheduler
member.summary:
Background scheduler that runs small periodic checks in-app: - PPM generator: create work orders for due preventive plans - Calibration alerts: 30/14/7 days before next_due - PQ renewal alerts: before validation_sets.pq_renewal_due - Low stock alerts: parts where stock < min_stock_alert
Notes: - Designed to be idempotent: uses external_ref markers to avoid duplicate WOs - Safe: catches all exceptions; never crashes UI - Lightweight: one pass on startup, then every 30 minutes

member.name: T:YasGMP.Services.DatabaseService
member.summary:
Partial extension of providing **compatibility overloads** for component CRUD. These forward to robust, schema-tolerant implementations here, removing any dependency on other overloads that may not exist in the current build.
• Intentionally uses positional parameters to avoid CS1739 issues with named args.
• Logs extended-context actions via for traceability.
• Uses reflection-safe getters defined on to stay tolerant to model drift.
Core ADO.NET gateway for MySQL with parameterized helpers and canonical audit writer. Partial class; other functional areas live in separate extension/partial files.
High‑level overview and usage guide for .

remarks.para: The DatabaseService is the single, centralized data‑access component for YasGMP. It encapsulates safe MySQL access (via MySqlConnector), parameterized SQL execution, resilient retry patterns, schema‑tolerant parsing, and canonical audit logging to system_event_log. It is intentionally written as a partial class and organized in regions so the codebase can evolve by functional area (CAPA, Deviations, Documents, Incidents, Inventory, IoT, etc.) without losing discoverability.

item.description: Safety first — all commands are parameterized; no string interpolation of user data.
Schema tolerance — data mappers check column presence before assignment, allowing the service to run against newer/older dumps while alignment is in progress.
Canonical audit — sensitive actions log to system_event_log with rich context (table, record id, IP, device info, session id, severity) for 21 CFR Part 11 / Annex 11.
Transactions — helpers for batched SQL and delegate‑based work ensure atomicity with commit/rollback and uniform tracing.

remarks.para: Thread‑safety: Each operation opens its own and disposes it deterministically; the service itself is stateless and safe to register as a singleton in DI.
Configuration: the constructor accepts a full ADO.NET connection string. For development, it can be injected from configuration key ConnectionStrings:MySqlDb. Example:
Typical usage from a ViewModel or service:

Error handling: all low‑level helpers (Execute*) write structured traces, wrap and rethrow errors, and also emit a diagnostic system event (DbError/TransactionFailed) with SQL and parameter fingerprints to aid forensics.
Performance: reads stream through for simplicity and robustness; heavy exports provide CSV paths instead of loading entire files in memory.

member.summary: Small, surgical, runtime migrations we can apply safely at app start. Specifically: reconcile machines triggers so "code" is never NULL.
Lightweight testing hooks that allow unit/integration harnesses to intercept database operations without requiring a live MySQL server. These are internal so they remain invisible to production code.

Canonical, schema-explicit system event logger. Inserts an Annex 11 / 21 CFR Part 11 style entry into system_event_log with rich context (table, record, field, before/after values, device, session, severity). Backward compatible with legacy named arguments (eventCode/table) and the positional overload.
member.param: User id associated with the event (nullable).
Event type (e.g., CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, LOGIN). If null/empty and is provided, that value is used.
Module or sub-system that triggered the event (nullable).
Primary key of the affected record (nullable).
Severity level (info, warn, error, critical). Default is info.
Legacy alias for eventType used if eventType is null/empty.
Legacy alias for tableName used if tableName is null.
Cancellation token for the async operation.

member.name: T:YasGMP.Services.DatabaseServiceUserOpsExtensions
member.summary:
Extension helpers for related to User operations.
⚠️ This file intentionally does not re-declare the canonical audit writer (LogUserAuditAsync) that already exists in . Doing so prevents CS0121 “ambiguous call” errors across view-models and services.
What remains here are uniquely-named convenience wrappers that simply forward to the RBAC extensions. They’re safe to use from views/view-models without risking signature collisions.

member.summary:
ICapaAuditService – GMP/21 CFR Part 11 compliant interface for CAPA audit logging services.
✔ Defines contract for full traceability of CAPA actions (CREATE, UPDATE, CLOSE, DELETE, etc.).

member.summary:
IPpmAuditService — Ultra-robust, GMP/21 CFR Part 11-compliant interface for logging all Preventive Maintenance Plan (PPM) audit actions.
Supports immutable audit trails, digital signatures, role-based approvals, AI anomaly scoring, and full forensic traceability.
Integrates with and supports batch, advanced filtering, regulatory export, rollback, and IoT-linked events.

RBACService – GMP / 21 CFR Part 11 compliant Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) for YasGMP. Manages users, roles, permissions, delegations, approval workflow and audit-grade security events.

member.name: T:YasGMP.Helpers.AuditLogMapper
member.summary:
AuditLogMapper – Static helper for robust mapping between (DTO) and (domain model).
✅ Used for displaying, exporting, and forensically investigating audit log entries in GMP/Annex 11/21 CFR Part 11 compliant systems.
✅ Ensures safe, reliable transfer of all critical audit fields, including digital signatures, user, action, old/new values, and session metadata.
✅ Handles nulls, type conversions, and defensive fallback for maximum data integrity.

member.param: The instance to map. Must not be null.
